description_long: This is a bulbous terracotta vase with a wide, flared neck and a rounded body, supported by a circular base. The most striking feature is the presence of two sculpted human or animal heads attached symmetrically to the upper sides of the body, just below the neck. The surface is matte and shows signs of age, with a mottled, weathered appearance and some areas of discoloration. The heads are stylized, with simple facial features, and appear to be hand-formed, adding a decorative and possibly symbolic element. The vase is hollow, with an open top suitable for holding flowers or other items, and the interior is likely unfinished clay. This object would be a distinctive decorative piece, evoking an ancient or archaeological aesthetic.
description: A rounded terracotta vase with a flared neck and two sculpted heads on its shoulders, featuring a weathered, matte surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing both sculpted heads clearly flanking the neck, with the wide opening and rounded body visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one of the sculpted heads in profile, with the curvature of the body and neck emphasized.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of both heads and the full roundness of the vase's body, with less detail on the faces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the other sculpted head, mirroring the profile seen in view 1.
